text:
The feedback is a genuine sharpening, not a competing view. The epic already said "the host owns all I/O" but never named the *boundary principle* that makes that non-arbitrary — **Trellis owns decisions; the host owns observations and effects.** That framing does real work: it gives an unambiguous test for what's a canonical input (a fact the world hands you: process died, LLM returned text, relay accepted the event, clock ticked) versus what Trellis derives (session is `working`, this exact 30315 should publish, this subscription should close). It's also fully consistent with the existing cost-model caveat about keeping raw transcripts/event bodies out of the graph. [...] The split-brain point is the most valuable part and was genuinely missing: partial adoption is strictly *worse* than the status quo, because two systems then disagree about the same rows and each believes it's authoritative. That's not a nuance — it's a hard constraint on how slices promote.
 [...] ### `InputFact` variants (each doc-comments the real writer it will replace)
- `SessionStarted{session_id, channel_h, agent_pubkey, pid, at}` → replaces `state::sessions::Store::register_session`/`upsert_session_row`
- `TurnStarted{session_id, at}` → replaces `set_working(id, working=true, turn_started_at)`
- `TranscriptWindowCaptured{session_id, window_hash, at}` → replaces `set_session_transcript` (carries hash, not text)
- `DistillCompleted{session_id, window_hash, title, activity, at}` → replaces `set_session_distill(id, title, activity, last_distill_at)`
- `TurnEnded{session_id, at}` → replaces `set_working(id, working=false, ..)`
- `RelayEventObserved{event_id, kind, channel_h, pubkey, at}` → replaces `state::events::Store::insert_event` (header fields only)
- `RelayPublishAccepted{local_id, event_id, accepted, at}` → replaces `outbox::Store::mark_published`/`mark_failed`
- `ProcessExited{session_id, pid, at}` → replaces the `runtime` `pid_alive` watcher calling `mark_dead`
- `ClockTick{at}` → replaces ad-hoc `now`/heartbeat reads in the runtime loop
